Composite Armor halves the final Penetration). Fuel-Air Explosive: This bomb covers its burst area with an explosive gas, which is sparked to cause a gigantic fireball/shockwave. It is the most destructive explosive in the world, short of thermonuclear ordnance. Any target caught in the burst area suffers a Penetration attack of 10. Only sealed armor offers any armor protec­ tion or resistance! MAXIMUM METAL Guided: This option allows the bomb to be remote-controlled, as per the bombing rules. Incendiary: Anything flammable within the bomb 's burst area catches fire. Treat as a flamethrower attack that lasts 20 turns or until the burning chemical is removed from contact (2d10 damage/Penetration 1 per turn). Napalm: Jellied gasoline on fire, this is the most hideous bomb weapon. Anyone or any­ thing caught in the burst area is covered in napalm, and sustains a Penetration 3 hit every turn for 20 turns. The stuff burns in water, too, so immersion is no help. Only sealed armor pro­ vides any protection from this attack. LA5ER5 Vehicular lasers are primarily restricted to space warefare. Inside the atmosphere, rail­ guns and electrothermal-enhanced weapons have the lion's share of developmental funds. At the moment, the only viable battlefield laser weapon is the Painting Laser, used to guide laser-guided weaponry. It can be blocked by anti-laser aerosols and smoke.
